game_info.title: "INITIATION",
game_info.concept: **异常;** "异常" is a single-player puzzle game where players program the AI of robotic units to complete level objectives. In each sequential level, players interact with a visual programming interface, dragging and dropping command blocks or inputting code snippets to define robot behaviors such as "move forward 3 units," "attack nearest enemy," or "wait 2 seconds." After programming, players tap a "Run Simulation" button to observe their robots execute the AI, with success determined by eliminating all enemies or reaching a designated exit point. Progression involves unlocking new levels that introduce additional robot types, environmental hazards, and increasingly complex enemy patterns, requiring players to craft sophisticated AI programs for multiple units to cooperate. Difficulty scales by increasing the number of robots to manage, the intricacy of required AI logic, and the precision needed in command sequencing, with failure occurring if any robot is destroyed or the objective is not met.
game_info.description: "EXCEPTION" is a strategic programming puzzle game where you act as an AI Operator. Your goal is to guide a robotic unit through glitch-ridden sectors to reach the extraction data-port. Instead of direct control, you must construct a sequence of commands (Move, Turn, Attack, Wait) in the programming phase. Once your sequence is ready, you compile and run the simulation. If your logic is flawless, the unit will navigate obstacles and neutralize threats to reach the exit. If you fail, debug your code and try again.
game_info.controls:
- Arrow Keys (Up/Down): Navigate the Command Palette to select instructions.
- Space: Insert the selected command into the program memory.
- Z: Delete the last command from memory (Undo).
- Enter: Execute/Compile the program (switches to Simulation mode).
- Esc: Pause/Unpause the game.
- R: Restart the current level (resets program).
- Shift: Fast-forward simulation (during execution).

TEST_1.test_description: Level 1 Completion Test. Validates that the programming logic, command queue execution, and win condition triggers work correctly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: The test detects Level 1, injects the specific sequence of commands (Move, Move, Move, Move, Move) required to walk straight to the exit, and simulates the Enter key to run the program.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The robot moves to the goal tile, triggers the win state, and the game logs transition to the next level or win screen.

TEST_1.expected: The robot moves to the goal tile, triggers the win state, and the game logs transition to the next level or win screen.

TEST_2.test_description: Collision and Error Handling Test. Validates that the robot handles walls and invalid moves gracefully without crashing.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Injects a command sequence that forces the robot to walk continuously into a wall, followed by an infinite loop of random turns.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The robot attempts to move, is blocked by the wall (staying in place), does not glitch out of bounds, and the game eventually fails the level due to not reaching the goal after the program ends.
TEST_2.description: Collision and Error Handling Test. Validates that the robot handles walls and invalid moves gracefully without crashing.

TEST_2.expected: The robot attempts to move, is blocked by the wall (staying in place), does not glitch out of bounds, and the game eventually fails the level due to not reaching the goal after the program ends.

TEST_3.test_description: Combat Logic Test. Verifies that the attack command correctly destroys enemies.
TEST_3.strategy_description: On a level with an enemy (Level 2), inject commands to move adjacent to the enemy, turn to face it, and execute the Attack command.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The enemy entity is removed from the game state, and the robot can occupy the previously blocked tile.
TEST_3.description: Combat Logic Test. Verifies that the attack command correctly destroys enemies.
